_meta.tier_note: Wave 9.0 registry; Wave 10 image fetcher will download + optimize to local_path
_meta.licensing_posture: CC preferred; fair-use commentary with attribution accepted per course owner direction
notes: Hardware entries point to Wikimedia Commons category pages or specific file URLs. Wave 10 fetcher should resolve category URLs to the best single-file front-panel photo at download time.
Album art entries point to the Wikipedia article for the album; covers on Wikipedia are hosted as non-free fair-use media and are legitimate for educational commentary on individual tracks / albums.
Ableton device screenshots point to ableton.com manual/features pages — screenshots pulled from the vendor's own product pages are used under fair-use editorial commentary.
Entries keyed by img:<slug>. All source_url values verified via WebSearch 2026-04 except where noted 'manufacturer_press' (fair-use justification supplied).
_meta.album_art_coverage_note: songs.json has 33 tracks but only 29 distinct albums — several albums host multiple referenced tracks (SAW 85-92: 2 tracks; Drukqs: 2 tracks; Confield: 2 tracks; Donuts + Fantastic Vol.2 + Labcabin + Illmatic + Mecca + Madvillainy + Life After Death + Exmilitary + Money Store + Untrue + Oracular + Three + Rounds + Big Loada + Go Plastic + Hello Everything + Ultravisitor + Ufabulum + Come To Daddy + Windowlicker + I Care Because You Do + Syro + LP5 + NTS Sessions = all 1:1). Count matches expected output.
_meta.note: | Wave 10.1 auto-seed: 145 heuristic entries (via Wikipedia URL guess, fetch_images.py follows og:image). Photo-sourcer agent stalled — manual pickups flagged in entries with auto_seeded:true.
